mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon. This fundamental strength underpins equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not consistently positive (e.g., average return < 0.1%) when UNRATE is below its 10-year historical median, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a short horizon as market participants react to strong economic data.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and housing starts (HOUST) are increasing, it signals an accommodative monetary environment supporting real estate activity, which often precedes broader economic growth and positive equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/-0.5%) in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS is low and HOUST growth is strong, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a bottoming process and potential recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor sentiment improves and expectations for future earnings rise.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases during a contraction but S&P 500 20-day returns remain negative for more than two consecutive quarters in such a regime, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a weakening labor market, signaling an impending economic slowdown or recession, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits are expected to decline.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 over a 3-month average, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL) allows central banks to maintain accommodative monetary policy, which supports higher equity valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with CPIAUCSL remaining below 2% over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and unemployment (UNRATE) is falling, it indicates a robust and expanding economy with strong aggregate demand and full employment, which is highly supportive of corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 25% of periods where both GDP growth is strong and unemployment is falling, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs and can slow economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a significant FEDFUNDS hike, the hypothesis is rejected.
